hooks.command: echo 'Blocked: git push is not allowed. Only the user pushes to remote.' >&2; exit 2
echo 'Blocked: git reset --hard is destructive. Use a safer alternative or ask the user.' >&2; exit 2
echo 'Blocked: git checkout -- discards uncommitted changes. Ask the user first.' >&2; exit 2
echo 'Blocked: git clean -f removes untracked files permanently. Ask the user first.' >&2; exit 2
echo 'Blocked: rm -rf is destructive. Use a targeted rm or ask the user.' >&2; exit 2

hooks.prompt: You are a verification checker. Review the conversation context provided in $ARGUMENTS. Determine: did Claude edit or write any code files in this turn (using Edit, Write, or NotebookEdit tools) AND did Claude NOT run a verification command (cargo fmt, cargo clippy, cargo test, npm test, pytest, ruff, eslint, or similar lint/test commands) before stopping? If Claude edited code but did not run any verification, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Code was edited but no verification (fmt/lint/test) was run. Run verification before committing."}. If no code was edited, or verification was already run, respond with {"ok": true}. Only flag when files were actually edited and verification was clearly skipped.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your attention" with title "Claude Code"'
